description: Quantum Security Expert — Zero to Enterprise-Deliverable Self-Study Track

sessions.title: Genesis — Project Setup + Phase 1 Research
sessions.summary: Created project structure, init skill, deployed ProfTeam for Phase 1 research.
key_learnings: Holy Trinity workflow established
Training knowledge sufficient for crypto math

sessions.title: Phase 1 Implementation + Phase 2 Research
key_learnings: 3 test vector errors from agents (fixed)
Implementation order matters

sessions.title: Marathon — Complete All 8 Phases + Holy Trinity Audit
sessions.summary: Completed all remaining phases (2-8), built 16 modules with 5,501 LOC and 127 tests. Deployed 29+ research agents across 5 master PDFs. Ran full Holy Trinity audit with 4 diagnostic agents finding 120 issues. Fixed 10 CRITICALs and 6 HIGHs. All fixes independently verified. Added AES-GCM, RFC 6979 ECDSA, C/C++ scanner patterns, BB84 privacy amplification.
deliverables_completed: ecc.py (secp256k1, P-256, ECDH, ECDSA with RFC 6979)
Holy Trinity audit: 10 CRITICALs + 6 HIGHs fixed
key_learnings: extended_gcd must use abs() for negative inputs
PKCS#7 unpad needs hmac.compare_digest to prevent padding oracle
RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 decrypt must scan ALL bytes (Bleichenbacher prevention)
E91 sifting must match on measurement ANGLES not basis indices
QRNG on AerSimulator is classical PRNG — must document clearly
HTML reports need html.escape() on all user data (XSS in security tools is ironic)
LWE n=64 gives only ~30-bit security — must warn users
AES-GCM GHASH uses GF(2^128) with irreducible R=0xE1...
RFC 6979 deterministic ECDSA nonces prevent Sony PS3 class attacks
Risk scoring needs logarithmic scaling to avoid saturation

sessions.title: v2 Enhancements — ML-KEM + Ed25519 + Scanner v2 + pytest
sessions.summary: Built ML-KEM (FIPS 203) with NTT and all 3 parameter sets, Ed25519 (RFC 8032) with extended coordinates, pqc-scanner v2 with SARIF output and certificate chain scanning. Migrated all 14 test files to pytest with parametrize/markers/conftest. Holy Trinity audit with 3 parallel agents found 32 issues, fixed 16 CRITICALs/HIGHs. Total: ~9,200 LOC, 353 tests.
deliverables_completed: ed25519.py (RFC 8032: keygen, sign, verify — 386 LOC, 24 tests)
Holy Trinity audit: 6 CRITICALs + 10 HIGHs fixed across 3 modules
key_learnings: Ed25519 'positive' root means x%2==0 (even), not odd — auditor false positive
ML-KEM NTT is 128-point (scaling by 3303=128^-1 mod Q), not 256-point
FIPS 203 CBD uses LSB-first bit ordering per byte — big-endian breaks interop
FIPS 203 G(d||k) domain separation is needed across parameter sets
hmac.compare_digest required for CCA2 security in FO transform
SARIF URIs must be relative when uriBaseId is %SRCROOT%
SARIF rule IDs cannot contain spaces — sanitize fallback IDs
SHAKE-128 XOF extension must request longer output, not re-hash same input
Verify RFC test vectors against cryptography library before trusting web sources

sessions.title: v3 Scanner + Consumer App + Sentinel Spinoff
sessions.summary: Extended session: v3 scanner (AST Python scanning, dependency scanner for 6 ecosystems, compliance mapping for CNSA 2.0/NIST/PCI DSS, executive PDF reports, GitHub Action). Holy Trinity audit on Phases 1-4 (13 fixes). Built consumer GUI (enigma_app.py) with sidebar nav and Psi branding. Built Sentinel spinoff (system security auditor, 9 modules, 13 MB .exe). User chose Path B: go deep on quantum security niche.
deliverables_completed: AST-based Python scanner (import resolution, call analysis, string detection)
Compliance mapper (CNSA 2.0, NIST IR 8547, PCI DSS 4.0)
ML-KEM key serialization (FIPS 203 encoding, all 3 levels)
Quantum hardware module (noise model, 5 circuits, ~98% avg)
Consumer GUI (enigma_app.py, sidebar, Psi branding, system scan)
Enigma.exe v3 (128 MB, all features, Psi icon)
Strategic decision: Path B — deep quantum security niche
key_learnings: tonelli_shanks(0,p) was unreachable dead code — move a==0 check before legendre_symbol
pollard_rho should use secrets not random for CSPRNG consistency
RSA OAEP decrypt must accumulate all error checks before raising (constant-time)
Noise model must use post-transpile basis gates (rz/sx/x not u1/u2/u3/h)
ReadoutError API is separate from depolarizing_error for measurement noise
Shor N=15 CSWAP circuit for a=7 actually implements a=8 (same order, same factors)
Consumer antivirus requires kernel drivers + WHQL signing — not buildable in Python
Path B (niche deep) beats Path A (broad) or Path C (compete with Defender) for solo builder
System hardening scanner is the adjacent product, not antivirus

sessions.title: v4.0 Wave 1-2: False Positive Elimination + Tree-sitter
sessions.summary: Wave 1: comment stripping, suppression, baseline diffing, CBOM 1.7, PyPI packaging. Wave 2: tree-sitter 7-language scanner, key size extraction, mode detection. 31+30 new tests.
key_learnings: String stripping too aggressive for Java APIs — default off
Tree-sitter 36x faster than language-specific parsers

sessions.title: v4.0 Wave 3-5: Compliance + Auto-fix + Enterprise Features
sessions.summary: Wave 3: CNSA deadlines, QARS scoring, lock files. Wave 4: auto-fix engine. Wave 5: readiness grade, quality gates, YAML rules, dashboard, history. CLI fully wired. Real-world scans on requests/flask.
key_learnings: SSL Labs grading methodology is THE feature enterprises understand
Quality gates are what makes CI/CD adoption possible

sessions.title: Production Readiness: Benchmark + Scale + Hardening
sessions.summary: Added 6 languages (13 total), IaC scanning, reachability analysis, backward slicing, migration estimator. Precision benchmark: 96.2%P/100%R/98%F1. Scale test: Django 7K files in 12s. Error hardening 10/10. Corporate PDF with Enigma E logo. Competitive analysis: 30 tools, #1 position.
key_learnings: 96.2% precision achievable with comment stripping + AST
32MB for 7K file scan is very lean
User wants REAL products not copies — benchmark everything

sessions.title: Enterprise Readiness + Market Validation
sessions.summary: Mega session: 10 quality tasks + 11-bug code audit + 4-agent marketing strategy + 3 cutting-edge features (AI triage, SBOM enrichment, call graph). 11 research agents, 340+ web searches. Tests: 499→562. LOC: 16K→26K. Pricing set: $749/$1,499/mo. Complete GTM plan with real buyer targets.
deliverables_completed: 6 tree-sitter languages upgraded to real AST (C#, Ruby, PHP, Kotlin, Swift, Scala)
Tier 2 auto-fix: real code transforms for Python/Java/Go/C#/Kotlin
Risk scores calibrated against NIST/ETSI/BSI (20 algos, 13 key sizes)
SQLite history backend (replaces JSON, multi-project, CSV export)
Enigma.exe v4 (23 MB, all features, 13 tree-sitter grammars)
VS Code extension compiled and packaged (pqc-scanner-4.0.0.vsix)
Docker image (multi-stage, CLI + API modes, docker-compose.yml)
Code audit: 11 CRITICAL/IMPORTANT bugs fixed (severity inversion, XSS, API auth, etc.)
Marketing strategy: complete GTM with 10 target companies, pricing, channels
AI Triage module (ai_triage.py): Claude API false positive detection
SBOM Enrichment module (sbom_enrichment.py): CycloneDX PQC annotations
Call Graph module (callgraph.py): pyan3 interprocedural crypto tracing
key_learnings: PQC market gap is real: no tool combines 16-lang AST + auto-fix + CBOM + compliance
5 Tier 1 purchase drivers: CNSA compliance, NIST mapping, CBOM, estimator, exec PDF
SandboxAQ ($5.75B) scans infrastructure NOT source code — different product category
IBM CBOMkit only 2-3 languages — Enigma's 16 is a genuine differentiator
Risk score calibration needs empirical basis — referenced NIST/ETSI/BSI for defensibility
Tree-sitter node types vary wildly by language (Kotlin: number_literal not integer_literal)
Severity enum min() not max() for escalation (CRITICAL=index 0 is lowest)
RSA CNSA 2.0 deadline for signing was Dec 2025 — OVERDUE, not 2030 networking deadline
CISOs don't trust cheap security tools — $749/mo signals enterprise-grade
Snyk had great usage and no revenue for a full year — patience required on SaaS
Black Hat Arsenal is FREE and exposes tool to 22,000+ security pros — best ROI channel
GSA PQC Buyer's Guide names ACDI tools as use case #1 — exact Enigma product category

sessions.title: Feature Completion + Direct Marketing Intelligence
sessions.summary: Holy Trinity on 3 remaining limitations (callgraph, reachability, dashboard): 21/35→30/35 in single pass. Honest product assessment. 3 parallel research agents produced verified buyer list (7 consulting firms, 5 defense, 8 finance, 9 events, 10 thought leaders). Direct_Marketing.pdf generated.
deliverables_completed: Multi-language call graph (13 languages via tree-sitter, was Python-only)
Tree-sitter AST reachability (replaced regex, all 13 languages)
Team dashboard (scan history, SVG trends, team activity, project comparison, delta view)
Enigma.exe v4 rebuilt (46 MB, all features)
key_learnings: Tree-sitter _extract_call_name must not collide with Python AST _extract_call_name (rename to _extract_ast_call_name)
Best buyer persona: small security consulting firms (5-20 people) doing PQC assessments, not enterprise CISOs directly
DEF CON Demo Labs and Black Hat Arsenal REQUIRE open-source — need community edition
Window of differentiation is now through ~2028 before Snyk/Checkmarx add PQC modules
Solo developer is enterprise liability — sell to consultants who resell, not procurement teams
Consulting-first model ($15K-50K per engagement) has highest probability of Y1 revenue
CNSA 2.0 Jan 2027 deadline for new NSS acquisitions is the most immediate purchase driver

sessions.title: Tank & Angel — 65-Fix Audit + Commercial Launch Prep
sessions.summary: Mega session: 4-round Holy Trinity audit (12 agents, 65 issues found+fixed, 0 regressions). Competitor scan confirmed Enigma as best PQC source SAST. 30+ buyer contacts scored, 6 competitors identified+eliminated. Pricing pivoted to $2,497 productized scan. FAA RFI response drafted. 2 personalized cold emails + 8-touch sequences built using proven Braun/Lavender/Gong frameworks. Enterprise readiness validated (all outputs, edge cases, CLI UX).
deliverables_completed: 65 bug fixes across 13 files (compliance, security, performance, data correctness)
4-round Trinity audit convergence (12 agents, 0 remaining issues)
Competitor analysis (30+ tools, Enigma confirmed #1 for source SAST)
Buyer intelligence (30+ contacts, 15 deep-scored, top 3 identified)
FAA RFI response draft (Model B, ROM $170-325K)
Full 8-touch email sequences for both targets (opener to close)
Marketbot outreach package (landing page, DMs, demo script)
Enterprise readiness validation (SARIF, CBOM, dashboard, edge cases)
Pricing pivot to $2,497 productized scan service
key_learnings: 6 of 15 original targets launched competing products Jan-Feb 2026 — market moving fast
Only 2 genuine ICP matches from original list: Flack (Avella, 62/100) and Ivezic (Applied Quantum, 58/100)
Braun permission-based CTA + Lavender ultra-short = highest reply rate framework for security founder cold outreach
CNSA 2.0 software signing deadline is 2030 (exclusive use), NOT 2025 — was wrong in code
P-256 is 128-bit classical security, NOT 112-bit — was wrong in compliance notes
Go des.NewCipher is single DES (CRITICAL), NOT 3DES — was mislabeled
AES-128 is NOT deprecated under NIST IR 8547 at 2030 — that deadline applies to 112-bit classical algorithms only
Google announced 2029 PQC migration deadline March 25 2026 — urgency compressed 3 years
Symlink attacks can escape scan root via os.walk — must validate realpath
API returning server absolute paths is info disclosure — return user-supplied path instead
SalesLoft data: breakup email on Day 14 gets 2-3x reply rate of earlier touches
Woodpecker data: 50-125 word emails optimal, plain text beats HTML 3x for cold outreach
ts_scan_directory and _collect_source_files both needed 10K file caps (was uncapped)
O(n^2) in _find_crypto_nodes fixed with pre-built module index

sessions.title: Phase 9 Vault Blueprint — Godspeed + Cycle Refinement (Personal Post-Quantum Vault)
sessions.summary: User asked for the smartest, actually-hard-to-bypass personal quantum-safe data vault + footprint reduction approach. Deployed godspeed mode with 4 parallel Sonnet research agents (PQC tooling state, hardware/endpoint, adversarial reality, CRQC+footprint timeline) returning 42+ T1-T3 sourced findings. Invoked cycle skill for 3-pass blueprint refinement — compressed Cycle 1 via pre-gathered context (SL-006) + Cycle 2 single refinement agent on age/restic/cryptomator patterns, OWASP Argon2id, zeroize-python, python-fido2 three-factor unlock. Produced 30-page PDF blueprint with concrete Python signatures, DevTeam 7 Laws audit (32/35), performance budget, risk matrix, session-level roadmap 9.1-9.7, test strategy. Wrote phase9_session_handoff.md resume doc with 5 explicit build paths. Phase 9 code scaffold NOT started — awaiting user greenlight.
deliverables_completed: research/Enigma_Phase9_Vault_Cycle_Blueprint.pdf (30 pages, 74.4 KB, 14 sections + 2 appendices, zero BifrostPDF API errors)
tools/generate_phase9_cycle_blueprint.py (~630 lines, regenerator with dynamic path resolution)
research/phase9_session_handoff.md (resume doc, 5 build paths A-E, critical reminders, expected file tree)
Memory files: user_quantum_defense_goals.md, project_enigma_structure.md, MEMORY.md index
Godspeed state checkpoint + learnings entry (structured v2.0 format)
Cycle state checkpoint + learnings entry with cross-skill relevance tags
SL-055 cross-skill promotion: prior-phase audit files as anti-pattern source for new-phase blueprints
Architecture verdict delivered: YES genuinely hard to bypass in 2026 for non-nation-state threat model
key_learnings: Argon2id m=64 MiB t=3 p=1 is OWASP 2025/2026 baseline-plus — NOT m=256 MB t=4 p=4 (parallelism causes timing leaks; hardware YubiKey lockout is real brute-force defense)
X25519 + ML-KEM-768 (X-Wing pattern) is the real-world hybrid default shipped by Chrome/Cloudflare/AWS/Apple — NOT ML-KEM-1024
age Recipient/Identity two-method interface split is the right abstraction for encryption CLIs — steal wholesale
Cryptomator chunk-index-as-AAD pattern prevents reorder/splice without separate MAC pass
Duress vault should be a second SLIP-0039 share set, NOT a branching flag — structurally invisible to attacker reading disassembly
zeroize-python v1.1.2 is Rust-backed and the only credible memory-wiping option in Python; bytearray throughout; ~2.6 MB mlock ceiling
python-fido2 2.1.1 (Jan 2026) HmacSecretExtension is the canonical three-factor unlock path; correct KDF chain: passphrase -> Argon2id -> salt1 -> YubiKey HMAC -> HKDF -> KEK -> unwrap master
EUCLEAK CVE-2024-45678 breaks ALL YubiKey firmware < 5.7 via EM side-channel — 5.7+ mandatory
TEE.Fail (late 2025, sub-$1K DDR5 bus interposer) breaks Intel SGX/TDX and AMD SEV-SNP attestation with physical access
US 5th Amendment protects alphanumeric passphrase in most circuits (Utah 2024); UK RIPA Part III Section 49 = 2-5 years prison for refusing key disclosure (no equivalent protection)
Mosca 2026 CRQC 10-year probability = 28-49% (highest ever recorded); 15-year = 50%+ per 69% of experts; act now for 20-year-lifetime personal data
Pre-existing phase audit files (e.g. audit_phases6_8.md) are a goldmine of 'don't do this' anti-patterns for extending-phase blueprints — always check before writing new phase
SL-012 cycle compression confirmed again: when research consensus is strong across 4+ agents, Cycle 1 collapses to rapid inventory and Cycle 2 needs only targeted refinement
For personal security architecture research: 4-agent split (tooling + hardware + adversarial + legal/timeline) + 1 refinement agent (comparable-project patterns) is the optimal pipeline

Google Chrome X25519MLKEM768 is TLS 1.3 default since late 2024/early 2025; Cloudflare > 1/3 human HTTPS traffic using hybrid PQC by March 2025; Signal PQXDH default + SPQR Triple Ratchet (Oct 2025) extends PQC to ongoing sessions
liboqs 0.15.0 (Nov 14 2025) is current; 'not production quality for TLS integration' per upstream but core KEM ops are solid; wrap in own abstraction per SL-034

Incogni is first independently audited data broker deletion service (Deloitte Aug 2025); combo with Optery covers ~800 brokers; CA DELETE Act DROP system goes mandatory Aug 2026 (45-day deletion, $200/day/request fines)
topics_studied: Qubes OS 4.3 + ThinkPad T480 Libreboot 26.01 anti-evil-maid
GrapheneOS duress PIN + Signal PQXDH on mobile
US vs UK compelled decryption law + foregone conclusion doctrine
Mosca inequality 2026 values + CRQC timeline (Piani survey)
Python memory safety: zeroize-python, mlock, bytearray discipline
python-fido2 2.1.1 HmacSecretExtension API for three-factor unlock
Rosenpass + WireGuard PQC handshake + Tailscale adoption
California DELETE Act DROP system (Aug 2026 mandatory)
LLM training data permanence (Anthropic/Google/OpenAI opt-out scope)
